# Coordinated inauthentic behaviour on Facebook? A typology of manufactured attention

**Authors:** Richard Rogers, Nicola Righetti  
**Year:** 2025  
**URL:** https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/10.1177/29768624251369784  
**Evidence type:** Empirical coordination-network analysis

## Claim supported

Timed repeated co-sharing can reveal manufactured attention networks beyond politics, including commercial and scam networks.

## Key result

Coordination is operationalized as accounts performing the same action together at least r times within a time window t.

## How it supports manufactured traction

This source strengthens the argument that digital systems can be moved by manipulated proxy signals. Use it as evidence for the mechanism, not as proof that every campaign succeeds.
